why: getPassword() returns nested user.getPassword() (which is null after Redis round-trip) instead of the top-level password field the constructor was given; auth password check reads this and fails on cache hit
@JSONField(serialize=false) on password means it is dropped when JwtUserDto is serialized to Redis, so the cached login data has no password and the stale cache fails the password check
the standalone password field carries the real password into the cache but is never used by getPassword(); making auth read this serialized field is the likely fix surface
